$comment: Cross-language: technique hint appended to T2/T3 prompts per dominant mutation kind. Shared by mutagen-qa (Python) and mutagen-qa-js. Update in one place to avoid drift.
comparison: boundary tests around the operands (values just below, equal to, and just above the compared boundary)
arithmetic: off-by-one and sign-flip inputs that make the arithmetic result observable in the return value
constant: inputs that force the exact literal value to matter (pin the constant with equality assertions)
boolean: truth-table cases: exercise each branch of the boolean expression with contrasting operand values
return: equality assertions on the return value for a spread of inputs, especially the ones that hit each return statement
keyword: tests that observe control-flow effects (loop iteration counts, early-exit values, throw-vs-fallthrough)
call: arguments and return-value shapes for the mutated call site
other: small, targeted example-based tests that pin down the surviving mutation's observable behavior
